docs.title: Welcome to the Blueprint docs
docs.text:
Blueprints are customizable workflows that help developers build AI applications using open-source tools and models

These docs are your companion to mastering this Blueprint.

docs.title: Why Blueprints?
docs.text: Blueprints are more than starter code—they’re your gateway to building AI-powered solutions with confidence. With step-by-step guidance, modular design, and open-source tools, we make AI accessible for developers of all skill levels.

docs.title: extract_slide_timestamps(model, chunks_dir, slide_timestamps_prompt=SLIDE_TIMESTAMPS_PROMPT)
docs.text:
Identify and extract the timestamps of the slide changes in a chunk.

Process all chunks inside chunks_dir.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default model

The model to use to extract the timestamps.

required chunks_dir str

Directory containing the video chunks.

required slide_timestamps_prompt str

Prompt to use to extract the timestamps.

SLIDE_TIMESTAMPS_PROMPT

Returns:

Type Description

None. Writes the timestamps to a JSON file for each chunk.

Source code in src/slides_vqa/preprocess_video.py 
def extract_slide_timestamps(
    model, chunks_dir: str, slide_timestamps_prompt: str = SLIDE_TIMESTAMPS_PROMPT
):
    """Identify and extract the timestamps of the slide changes in a chunk.

    Process all chunks inside `chunks_dir`.

    Args:
        model: The model to use to extract the timestamps.
        chunks_dir: Directory containing the video chunks.
        slide_timestamps_prompt: Prompt to use to extract the timestamps.

    Returns:
        None. Writes the timestamps to a JSON file for each chunk.
    """
    chunks_dir = Path(chunks_dir)
    for chunk in chunks_dir.glob("*.mp4"):
        slide_timestamps = model.process_video(
            str(chunk), prompt=slide_timestamps_prompt
        )
        (chunks_dir / f"{chunk.stem}.json").write_text(json.dumps(slide_timestamps))

docs.title: merge_timestamps(chunks_dir, chunk_seconds)
docs.text:
Joins timestamps from multiple JSON files into a single list.

Assumes that the timestamp files are numerically ordered, like the output generated by extract_slide_timestamps.

Applies an offset to each timestamp based on the chunk it came from.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default chunks_dir str

Directory containing the JSON files.

The expected format of each JSON file is:

[
    {"timestamp": "01:00", "title": "First slide", "description": "This slide displays the outline of the talk."},
    ...
]
required chunk_seconds int

Duration of each chunk in seconds.

required

Returns:

Type Description

A list of merged timestamps.

Source code in src/slides_vqa/preprocess_video.py 
def merge_timestamps(chunks_dir: str, chunk_seconds: int):
    """Joins timestamps from multiple JSON files into a single list.

    Assumes that the timestamp files are numerically ordered, like
    the output generated by [extract_slide_timestamps][slides_vqa.preprocess_video.extract_slide_timestamps].

    Applies an offset to each timestamp based on the chunk it came from.

    Args:
        chunks_dir: Directory containing the JSON files.

            The expected format of each JSON file is:

            ```json
            [
                {"timestamp": "01:00", "title": "First slide", "description": "This slide displays the outline of the talk."},
                ...
            ]
            ```

        chunk_seconds: Duration of each chunk in seconds.

    Returns:
        A list of merged timestamps.
    """
    merged_timestamps = []
    file_list = sorted([str(f) for f in Path(chunks_dir).glob("*.json")])

    for file_index, filename in enumerate(file_list):
        chunk_offset_seconds = file_index * chunk_seconds

        with open(filename, "r") as f:
            data = json.load(f)

        for entry in data:
            relative_timestamp_str = entry["timestamp"]
            relative_seconds = time_to_seconds(relative_timestamp_str)
            absolute_seconds = relative_seconds + chunk_offset_seconds
            absolute_timestamp_str = seconds_to_time(absolute_seconds)

            merged_entry = {
                "timestamp": absolute_timestamp_str,
                "title": entry["title"],
                "description": entry["description"],
            }
            merged_timestamps.append(merged_entry)

    return merged_timestamps

docs.title: seconds_to_time(total_seconds)
docs.text:
Converts total seconds to MM:SS format.

Source code in src/slides_vqa/preprocess_video.py 
def seconds_to_time(total_seconds):
    """Converts total seconds to MM:SS format."""
    minutes = (total_seconds % 3600) // 60
    seconds = total_seconds % 60
    return f"{minutes:02d}:{seconds:02d}"

docs.title: split_video_into_chunks(input_video, output_dir, chunk_seconds)
docs.text:
Splits a video into chunks of a specified duration.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default input_video str

Path to the input video file.

required output_dir str

Directory where the chunks will be saved.

required chunk_seconds int

Duration of each chunk in seconds.

required Source code in src/slides_vqa/preprocess_video.py 
def split_video_into_chunks(input_video: str, output_dir: str, chunk_seconds: int):
    """
    Splits a video into chunks of a specified duration.

    Args:
        input_video: Path to the input video file.
        output_dir: Directory where the chunks will be saved.
        chunk_seconds: Duration of each chunk in seconds.
    """
    try:
        (
            ffmpeg.input(input_video)
            .output(
                str(Path(output_dir) / "%03d.mp4"),
                c="copy",
                map="0",
                segment_time=chunk_seconds,
                f="segment",
            )
            .run(capture_stdout=True, capture_stderr=True)
        )
    except ffmpeg.Error as e:
        print(f"stdout: {e.stdout.decode('utf8')}")
        print(f"stderr: {e.stderr.decode('utf8')}")
        raise e

docs.title: time_to_seconds(time_str)
docs.text:
Converts a timestamp string (MM:SS) to seconds.

Source code in src/slides_vqa/preprocess_video.py 
def time_to_seconds(time_str):
    """Converts a timestamp string (MM:SS) to seconds."""
    parts = list(map(int, time_str.split(":")))
    minutes, seconds = parts
    return minutes * 60 + seconds

docs.title: 🚀 Future Features & Contributions
docs.text: This Blueprint is an evolving project designed to grow with the help of the open-source community. Whether you’re an experienced developer or just starting, there are many ways you can contribute and help shape the future of this tool.

docs.title: 🛠️ Enhance the Blueprint
docs.text:

docs.title: 🎨 Extensibility Ideas
docs.text:
This Blueprint is designed to be a foundation you can build upon. By extending its capabilities, you can open the door to new applications, improve user experience, and adapt the Blueprint to address other use cases. Here are a few ideas for how you can expand its potential:

We’d love to see how you can enhance this Blueprint! If you create improvements or extend its capabilities, consider contributing them back to the project so others in the community can benefit from your work. Check out our Contributions Guide to get started!

docs.title: 💡 Share Your Ideas
docs.text: Got an idea for how this Blueprint could be improved? You can share your suggestions through GitHub Discussions.

docs.title: 🌍 Build New Blueprints
docs.text:
This project is part of a larger initiative to create a collection of reusable starter code solutions that use open-source AI tools. If you’re inspired to create your own Blueprint, you can use the Blueprint-template to get started.

Your contributions help make this Blueprint better for everyone 🎉
